text:
Once upon a quaint, sunlit afternoon in a small English village, there lived a kind witch. Her oven door never froze, and her cauldron boiled with just the right amount of magic. It was she who helped the villagers, not through frightful potions and ghoulish potions, but through spells of goodwill such as improving harvests or bestowing flowers that danced in the breeze.

On one peculiar afternoon, there was a strange commotion by the river. The fish were fleeing, panic in their watery eyes. It weren’t ghoulish creatures after them; it was the villagers themselves, chasing butterflies that buzzed with gossip!

The witch, seeing the chaos, hopped out of her cottage with a knowing chuckle. She cast a butterfly charm—something so simple yet effective. In a moment, the butterflies became a gaggle of friendly chatting creatures, leading the fish right back home.

The villagers were dumbfounded, having believed their own tales. Grateful, they
